provenance.notes: First-pass identifier terminology reference. This file documents known identifier spaces and conversion status; it does not derive mappings.

identifierKinds.label: bytes5 MoonCat ID
identifierKinds.notes: The contract source uses bytes5 cat IDs. The API landing pages document a valid MoonCat ID example for catId_or_rescueIndex. This file does not define the full catId parser or any reverse mapping.

identifierKinds.label: API original rescue index
identifierKinds.notes: The API landing pages document rescueIndex values from 0 through 25439 for catId_or_rescueIndex. Do not assume these values are the same as local rescue-order-index values until a derivation or mapping is verified.

identifierKinds.label: local rescue-order index
identifierKinds.notes: Local membership and rescue bucket files use indexKind rescue-order-index. These values are useful inside their local datasets but are not automatically API rescue indexes, token IDs, OpenSea IDs, bytes5 catIds, or contract call values.

identifierKinds.label: ERC-721 or wrapped token ID
identifierKinds.notes: This repository has not yet verified the token ID convention for ERC-721 or wrapped MoonCat surfaces. Do not fill token-facing IDs from local index arrays.

identifierKinds.label: OpenSea token ID
identifierKinds.notes: Marketplace token ID behavior has not been verified in this repository. Do not assume OpenSea IDs match rescue-order indexes, API rescue indexes, bytes5 catIds, or wrapped token IDs.

identifierKinds.label: Ethereum address
identifierKinds.notes: Used for owner/profile and owned-MoonCat API lookups. This file does not document checksum, lowercase, or ENS normalization behavior.

identifierKinds.label: accessory ID
identifierKinds.notes: API paths document accessoryId parameters. This pass does not define accessory ID format, range, contract origin, or validation rules.

knownParameters.notes: API landing pages document this parameter as accepting either a valid MoonCat ID or original rescue index.

conversionRules.notes: Do not assume equivalence. These conventions may prove to align, but this repository does not yet document the method.
A verified source query or parser-derived mapping is required before using local indexes as cat IDs.
Do not use local rescue-order indexes as token-facing IDs without a verified mapping.
Do not use local rescue-order indexes as OpenSea token IDs without a verified mapping.

relatedFiles.relationship: documents API parameters and endpoint usage
uses local rescue-order-index rescue/history bucket arrays
documents bytes5 cat ID generation facts from contract source

todos: Document the exact derivation method for local rescue-order-index arrays.
Verify whether API original rescue indexes and local rescue-order indexes align.
Verify token-facing ERC-721 or wrapped token ID conventions from primary sources.
Verify OpenSea token ID conventions before recording marketplace-specific IDs.
Document any source-backed conversion between bytes5 catIds and index-based identifiers.
